mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength and robust labor demand, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent PAYEMS prints show continued job growth post-2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) using a 6-month change in PAYEMS is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI growth accelerates rapidly, it signals persistent inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of monetary tightening which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation is currently subdued, but this mechanism was relevant in past cycles.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the YoY CPI growth trigger is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) concurrent with a near-zero Fed Funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates the Federal Reserve is 'behind the curve' on inflation, creating a risk of future aggressive tightening that negatively impacts forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:At decision time, UNRATE is 5.7% and FEDFUNDS is 0.11%, activating the trigger.
failure_condition: The combined signal (low UNRATE, low FEDFUNDS) fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate greater than 55% over a walk-forward test covering one full tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a robust rebound in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, driving corporate profits and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The US economy is in an expansionary phase following the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: During periods identified as post-shock recoveries, positive INDPRO momentum is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals an unexpected contraction in consumer spending, which constitutes a significant portion of GDP, leading to negative revisions in earnings forecasts and an immediate negative market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:The most recent RSAFS release showed a month-over-month decline.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive 5-day forward return in more than 50% of instances following a monthly retail sales drop of more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An ultra-low federal funds rate provides a persistent tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate for future cash flows and encouraging risk-taking behavior among investors. This effect is always-on while rates are near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has maintained a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) since 2008.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening consumer confidence and investment in a key cyclical sector, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacting equity markets over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: 6-month moving average of HOUST is declining for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce negative average 60-day returns in the 12 months following the activation of the housing starts decline signal.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth (INDPRO) outpaces retail sales growth (RSAFS), it signals a potential inventory glut where supply is exceeding final demand. This imbalance leads to future production cuts, lower corporate profits, and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month % change in INDPRO > 3-month % change in RSAFS
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year rolling backtest window.
